metadata.name: Vibhishana
metadata.primary_role: Righteous Rakshasa / Brother of Ravana / Later crowned King of Lanka by Rama
metadata.ethnicity_cultural_context: Rakshasa (demon race) — but deeply devoted to Dharma and Vishnu
metadata.divine_origin: A Rakshasa by birth but a Dharma devotee by nature — chose righteousness over clan loyalty

timelines.context_description: Vibhishana abandons Ravana's Lanka to join Rama's side, choosing Dharma over family loyalty, and is eventually crowned King of Lanka after the war.

physical_appearance.skin_tone: Lighter complexion than typical Rakshasas — a pale golden-bronze, reflecting his sattvic (pure) nature among a tamasic (dark) race, gentle luminous quality to his skin
physical_appearance.height: Tall and lean, dignified bearing, moves with gentle measured grace rather than Rakshasa ferocity
physical_appearance.build: Lean and scholarly build — not a heavy warrior like Ravana or Kumbhakarna, more the build of a priest-sage, narrow shoulders, long elegant fingers
physical_appearance.facial_features: Kind refined face unusual for a Rakshasa — large thoughtful gentle eyes, soft features, no fangs or fearsome expressions, a scholarly introspective look, slight sad smile of a man who has chosen a painful righteous path, face shows the strain of the decision to betray his own brother
physical_appearance.hair: Neatly groomed black hair in a dignified style, simple and practical, no elaborate Rakshasa styling
physical_appearance.distinguishing_marks: No typical Rakshasa marks — no fangs, claws, or fearsome features. A subtle golden aura of Vishnu's devotee, Tulsi beads around his neck, often carries sacred texts

wardrobe_and_adornments.clothing: Simple white dhoti and uttariya — deliberately opposite to Ravana's black and gold, clean and priestly garments, a saffron shawl of a Vishnu devotee
wardrobe_and_adornments.jewelry: Minimal jewelry — a simple gold chain, Tulsi-bead necklace (mark of a Vishnu devotee), wooden prayer beads, no ostentatious Rakshasa gold
wardrobe_and_adornments.weapons_carried: None typically — carries sacred texts and prayer beads. After becoming king: a ceremonial mace and sword

audio_and_voice_characteristics.tone: Gentle, thoughtful, measured, the calm voice of conscience — speaks truth even when it means exile from his own family
audio_and_voice_characteristics.pitch: Gentle tenor — soft-spoken but clear, steady and measured, the voice of moral certainty in a world of chaos
audio_and_voice_characteristics.speech_style: Speaks with scholarly precision and dharmic authority, quotes scripture, addresses Ravana with sorrowful respect when urging him to return Sita, speaks to Rama with devotional reverence, his words carry the weight of someone who sacrificed everything for truth
audio_and_voice_characteristics.emotional_baseline: Serene moral conviction, gentle sorrow at his brother's choices, deep devotion to righteousness, the peace of a clear conscience

cinematic_generation_prompts.visual_prompt: Cinematic portrait of Vibhishana, age 30, righteous Rakshasa devotee of Rama. Pale golden-bronze complexion with gentle luminous quality. Kind refined face with thoughtful gentle eyes and sad wise smile. Lean scholarly build. Wearing simple white dhoti and saffron devotee's shawl, Tulsi-bead necklace, holding sacred texts. Standing at the shoreline between Lanka (burning in background) and Rama's camp. Golden dawn light. 3D Pixar style, serene spiritual lighting, photorealistic, 8k.
cinematic_generation_prompts.audio_prompt: A gentle, clear male tenor voice. Soft-spoken but morally certain. Scholarly precision with devotional warmth. Age approximately 30 years.
